terms.definition: Information collected by counting, observing, or asking questions. Data is made up of numbers or facts that help us answer questions.
terms.exampleUsage: We collected data about the favourite sports of students in our class by asking each student.

terms.definition: Short lines used to count and record data one item at a time. Every 5th mark is drawn across the first 4 to make a bundle of 5 for easy counting.
terms.exampleUsage: We used tally marks to count how many red, blue, green, and yellow cars passed our school gate.

terms.definition: How many times something happens or appears. The number you get after counting all the items in a group.
terms.exampleUsage: The frequency of students who chose mango as their favourite fruit was 14.

terms.definition: A table that shows different groups or items along with their tally marks and total counts. It organises raw data neatly.
terms.exampleUsage: Our frequency table showed that 8 students walk to school, 12 come by bus, and 5 cycle.

terms.definition: A chart that uses small pictures or symbols to represent data. Each picture stands for a certain number of items.
terms.exampleUsage: In our pictograph, one book symbol stands for 5 books read by a student.

terms.definition: A small note on a pictograph that tells you how many items each picture represents.
terms.exampleUsage: The key on the pictograph said that one ball picture equals 3 balls.

terms.definition: A chart that uses rectangular bars of different heights or lengths to show and compare data. The taller the bar, the larger the value.
terms.exampleUsage: The bar graph showed that cricket had the tallest bar, meaning it was the most popular sport.

terms.definition: A straight line on a graph used to show information. A bar graph has two axes: the horizontal x-axis (going across) and the vertical y-axis (going up).
terms.exampleUsage: The names of fruits were written along the x-axis, and the number of students was on the y-axis.

terms.definition: The set of numbers placed along an axis of a graph. The numbers go up by equal steps like 2, 4, 6 or 5, 10, 15.
terms.exampleUsage: The scale on the bar graph went from 0 to 25, with marks at every 5.

terms.definition: A way of collecting data by asking people questions. You record each person's answer to find patterns.
terms.exampleUsage: We did a survey in our class to find out which animal each student likes the most.

terms.definition: The name given to a chart or graph that tells you what the data is about.
terms.exampleUsage: The title of the bar graph was 'Favourite Colours of Class 4 Students'.

terms.definition: A group or type that data is sorted into. Each bar or row in a chart represents one category.
terms.exampleUsage: In our favourite fruit survey, mango, apple, banana, and orange were the four categories.
